title: Fit Check Call (40 min) between Alan Thomas
meeting_title: Fit Check Call (40 min) between Leonid Shvorob and Alan Thomas

transcript.text: Hi, Alan.

Or something, right.

when I was already like so sleepy, so I couldn't wait here a little bit more.
Good.

usually C, usually CEOs.
Mm-hmm.
Okay, so it's like...
Usually as the, as the startups evolve, they get more mature, they need to fill out a CEO role.
Mm-hmm.
Yeah, I guess it's like they have some genius idea, but they're like scientists, and they know, don't know how to the business, and like the investors want some management team running it.
So that's why they need your help, right?
Exactly.
So, yeah, as you said, this is not so easy to track.
So how you usually find this type of clients?
How they usually come into you?

Um, it's, um, a lot of personal relationships, a lot of knowledge of the industry, a lot of personal outreach, um, some keeping in touch with people, tracking news on, um, LinkedIn, and then there's some very particular sites, um, one is called InnovoSource.com, which is sort of, um,
It's where, it's sort of the club of all the proof-of-concept funds and the seed funds that invest in these things.
So I used to work at the University of Chicago, where I did this from the university side, and got to know most of the folks in the space.
Yeah, think, like, you over decades in this game, so people, like, know you, and sometimes, like, they call in if they need, but not always.
Exactly.
Right?
Yeah.

So I think, for you, it's not a problem if, like, you have founders who, in this moment, need to see you, or you can easily talk with him, and with all your experience, like, I think, there's not a problem to, like, convince him that, that's, uh,
You can help, right?
None if I get in touch, but tracking all the news is a very sort of thin and inefficient market is definitely not so easy.
Yeah, so I think it's probably like 5 or 6K companies in the US or maybe less, maybe a bit more.
I don't know what the total number is, to be honest.
And I only really want to work for the good ones, because trying to sell Duff deals to the candidates is, you know, that doesn't work very well.
So, yeah, it looks like now is a lot of time going to that researching, sitting on this news.
Yeah, so it's maybe like half a day is researching, and then if something appears, it's like you already going to your network.
To find this like CUS or how it's like going?
It's mainly making sure people are aware of what I do.
By the time the news happens, it's usually too late.
So it's sort of a lot of perspective relationship building and reminding.
Yeah, makes sense.
So usually I think it's like in exec search, this kind of roles, it takes like cycle of maybe six months or more to fill the role or?
Yeah, about three to six months.
But fees I think should be good, like 60K plus or something.
Okay, so like for example, what's like your goal for this year?
Like.
Alright.
How many new placements you want to do?
Can you share with me?
So we get aligned.
Yeah, 8 to 10 would be good.
Yeah, those are good numbers.
So yeah, certainly I think I get when you're now.
So you have a very good experience in this kind of field, but it took too much time to monitoring, to keeping in touch with all these people you know.
And yeah, sometimes like waiting is a bit passive, right?
Right, exactly.
Yeah.
I mean, I won't say I've cracked it yet, although I'm getting closer.
Yeah.
So usually when we're working with clients, what we're doing, even if it's not like generic exec searches, if it's more targeting.
So first, we're identifying what is like early signals, not when all your roles go public, but when it's the step before.

Exactly so.
Yeah.
So that's the bad news.

I think.

So.

Who have very specific niche, so they are focusing, so that's why I reached out to you, because you are super specific and exactly the type of client that I enjoy the most, because it's very interesting to go deeper, and also delivery is also in a good place.

So I'm not a leading agency or something, usually what like this kind of folks do is just blasting the volume, and praying that it will like get something, but in kind of niches like yours, think timing, reputation is like butter and bread, so it's better to do less conversations, but best quality, than just sending a spreadsheet, of like leads, that's when you reach out to them, they don't know who you are, and so something like this, so that's not what I'm doing.

What is your history?
How did you get to what you're doing and to Bali?
Yeah, so most of my life, like in sales marketing and, you know, with all these technologies, this, and banking for the last few years.
Where are you from?
Yeah, originally, yeah, I guess.
Somewhere in Eastern Europe, Russia, Ukraine, something like that.
Yeah, I think my accent is too much Russian still.
Yeah, well, don't blame me for being in Bali.
So, what did you study at school?
You know, chemistry was my most dislike, dislikely subject.
I'm sorry.

Yeah, yeah, so I'm not good in this, I'm more good in math, some technical stuff, you know, but, yeah, I always, like, inspired, like, our, like, teachers who do all this super, you know, difficult biology, chemistry, so I think, oh, like, it's on my plate.
Okay, so I think you probably went in the direction of computers then, right?

Yeah, yeah, so what's now I'm trying to do, and it's, like, raising the gap, because now a lot of people, like you, who are very good professionals, but they don't take advantage of their, like, last technologies, then can easily just bring so much value, because if, like, all the startups don't know about you, it's, like, just sitting and waiting where they reach out to you, it's not bad, but it's a bit passive, so if we use all.
All this power that we have in the modern world, and just making a good connection in the right timing, when you exactly can help, I think it would be very good.
What do you think?
How do you do it?
Yeah, so as I said, first, when I start working with clients, if it's some generic or more generic signals, so it's already, we have all infrastructure.
If it's more niched, so usually we're talking, what forums are good, what like more niched, like bases, databases, we should monitor, and then we have the source of signals.

Yeah, sure.
So, yeah, I'm working with a bunch of recruiting, staffing agencies.
So some of them place in high senior roles.
I will send you later the more detailed information.
If you wanted to do your own research, take a look.

I

So when we start working, I cannot just put, you know, like three months, just, you know, you say like it can be six months to close the role.

So for three months, it's 9-12K.

I think you said you were talking to private equity, people responsible.

No, that's not what I mean.
And I wouldn't, I'm sure they wouldn't fit my niche if you did.
I'm just.
I'm about exactly the way how I caught your attention, so now we're talking.

that's...
A massive section of the industry.

So if the conversations you're referencing are real, I'm just curious what fields they were in and why there were no recruiters who the private equity folks could find that understood their business.
That just seems unlikely.

So exactly you, we're doing our research, we have our systems to call attention that bright people like you.
So it was pretty successful, but what's good when your niche is like narrow, as you mentioned, right?
So when we set up this,
System, it can bring you the right people all over the years.
So this is their benefits from this approach.
Okay, you're not really answering my question.
Yeah, actually, I'm not talking to private equity like last week.
So basically, we just make some copyrights that caught your attention, and I've been honest with you.
But exactly the same way, we bring in to our clients attention to their decision makers who are hiring.
So exactly the same system.
So, yeah, not going to lie.
Okay.
And is it just you?
Is it a solo practitioner, a solopreneur, or do you have a team?
Yeah, I have a small team that's helping me with some, you know, routine stuff.

Thank
Seven clients, you know, so I don't want to have a bigger team because I don't want targeted more generic niches because I'm really enjoying when working with some challenging, like, kind of niches.
So, kind of, we're talking now.
I hear you.
And do you have some magic sauce about how you're monitoring targeted news sources?
Mm-hmm.
Yeah, magic sauce.
Do you, are you, I mean, in the old days, people used to use Google smart searches.
Now we've got, like, AI, right?
Whatever that means.

So, you don't need to waste your time, and if, like, I don't know.

So I think people from those days would call it magic.
You're right.
Right, so, yeah, so that would put you in the category of the automated sales development representative space, right?
I see you very, very want to put me in some box, right?
Well, yes, I mean, this is marketing, right?
Like, what niche do you put in?
Like, there's a...

I can't remember, getvalley.ai or something like that.
Yeah, lot of websites in the internet, that's right.

The point is, they monitor the whole internet.
Particularly on the media, it's not the thing I can do.
I'm sorry, sorry.
I'm sorry, sorry.
I'm sorry, particularly on the social
And then have some algorithm that shows potential buying interest.

And then they claim they can train your voice into outreach.
And they will try to book calls for you.

Which is kind of sort of what you're...
Yeah, so what you're saying, know, these systems we're also using, like, in our work...
But I don't like to overwhelm my clients with a lot of spreadsheets they need to look in every morning.
think you have a lot of better things to do, right?
So usually...
Well, unless they're super-focused.
I want information, not data.
Yeah, so usually I'm doing all their dirty work with all my systems, sometimes myself, just to give you a couple of right conversations that will lead to deals.
I don't want to give you a lot of dashboards, like spreadsheets, like, you know, flashing lights, because this is my, like, kitchen, but I don't want to overwhelm you with all this information.
I think that's not the best way to work with you, right?
So how do you present me with the information?

Yeah, so usually, as I said, first, we will have some conversation with you, when you give me all information, all the data sources, like, that you're monitoring right now, then I will do my research to find the similar resources that we will be monitoring, if they exist in the internet.

And then, when I get the signal that this company will need a new CEO, I will reach now to them, I will confirm that they really need it, and if it's the right moment, then, and only then, I will connect them to you.
I will, like, present you, and then, you can take it from here.
So, less noise, more clarity, and, yeah, think, usually, it will look better.
Got it.
Okay.
So, do you have...
So, how it sounds?
...more information?
Other than what is on your, the website I was able to find?
Yeah, sure.
How about like this?
I can send you my case studies that's in like similar or a space.
You can take a look at them.
So, yeah, let's say you will be satisfied with what you see and would you be able to start like this month or next month or what's your big plan is?
You're going for the close, Leonid.
I couldn't tell you.
I'm still in exploration mode.
I definitely have a need, but I need to be sure I understand the services and that it'll work for me.
Yeah, so usually the best way to understand something is start something, right?
When we start working, you will understand something very clearly.
And when you will have the first, like, conversations, it will be super clear, I think.
I know that you're...
It can also be a massive distraction and not a good use of money.
So, yeah, so about distraction, as I said, I don't want to overwhelm you information.
Usually, when we start working with a new client, it's just one, two hours peak of call when I just exploring everything I need.
And then it's maybe once or two weeks or once a week when I just wrote in the right signals and, like, some updates.
just that.
Good.
Well, send me the case studies and then I'll let you know if I want to talk again soon or I need to continue my research.
Okay.
I will send you just...
That's when we finish the call, so you can just check your WhatsApp or email when you prefer to communicate.
That'll be great.
Thank you, Leonid.
Okay, awesome.
So, can you give me the answer about would you like to continue or should we make another call like this, I think, next week in, like, soon or you will need to think more and more analyze?
If I understand what you're doing and the value you could bring and confidence that you could reach out effectively to my targets, we can talk again next week for sure.
But I would like to see the case studies first.

Okay, I will send you case studies, if you've seen this enough for you, you can just send me Leo, give me a little bit more info, and I can give you more, you know, specific signals that, in my opinion, would make sense to monitor in your market.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Evaluate SystemHustle's lead-gen service for Alan Thomas's exec search firm.](https://fathom.video/share/qM2aa5qgrqsD-aWVUJhfwTQxVLCHxQRe?tab=summary&timestamp=49.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Alan's Niche:** Places CEOs at early-stage (seed–Series A) university life science spinouts, a high-value but inefficient market. Goal: 8–10 placements/year at \~$60k+ fees.](https://fathom.video/share/qM2aa5qgrqsD-aWVUJhfwTQxVLCHxQRe?tab=summary&timestamp=49.0)
  - [**SystemHustle's Pitch:** Automates monitoring of niche signals (e.g., new funding) to identify hiring needs before they go public, providing exclusive access to decision-makers.](https://fathom.video/share/qM2aa5qgrqsD-aWVUJhfwTQxVLCHxQRe?tab=summary&timestamp=461.0)
  - [**Pricing Model:** A 3-month pilot retainer of $3k–$4k/month ($9k–$12k total), with a goal of delivering 5–6 qualified conversations.](https://fathom.video/share/qM2aa5qgrqsD-aWVUJhfwTQxVLCHxQRe?tab=summary&timestamp=1080.0)
  - [**Outcome:** Alan is interested but skeptical, having already explored automated tools like Valley.ai. He requested case studies to validate the service's effectiveness before committing to a follow-up call.](https://fathom.video/share/qM2aa5qgrqsD-aWVUJhfwTQxVLCHxQRe?tab=summary&timestamp=1523.0)

## Topics

### Alan's Business & Challenges

  - [**Niche:** Places CEOs at early-stage (seed–Series A) university life science spinouts.](https://fathom.video/share/qM2aa5qgrqsD-aWVUJhfwTQxVLCHxQRe?tab=summary&timestamp=49.0)
  - [**Market:** Inefficient and difficult to track; news breaks often come too late.](https://fathom.video/share/qM2aa5qgrqsD-aWVUJhfwTQxVLCHxQRe?tab=summary&timestamp=302.0)
  - [**Current Process:** Relies on personal network, industry knowledge, and manual monitoring of sites like `InnovoSource.com`.](https://fathom.video/share/qM2aa5qgrqsD-aWVUJhfwTQxVLCHxQRe?tab=summary&timestamp=190.0)
  - [**Goal:** 8–10 placements per year at \~$60k+ fees.](https://fathom.video/share/qM2aa5qgrqsD-aWVUJhfwTQxVLCHxQRe?tab=summary&timestamp=393.0)

### SystemHustle's Proposed Solution

  - [**Service:** Automated monitoring of niche signals to identify hiring needs early, enabling proactive outreach.](https://fathom.video/share/qM2aa5qgrqsD-aWVUJhfwTQxVLCHxQRe?tab=summary&timestamp=461.0)
  - [**Value Prop:** Delivers qualified conversations, not raw data, to avoid overwhelming the client.](https://fathom.video/share/qM2aa5qgrqsD-aWVUJhfwTQxVLCHxQRe?tab=summary&timestamp=1672.0)
  - [**Process:**](https://fathom.video/share/qM2aa5qgrqsD-aWVUJhfwTQxVLCHxQRe?tab=summary&timestamp=796.0)
    1.  [Define target signals and data sources.](https://fathom.video/share/qM2aa5qgrqsD-aWVUJhfwTQxVLCHxQRe?tab=summary&timestamp=796.0)
    2.  [Automate daily monitoring.](https://fathom.video/share/qM2aa5qgrqsD-aWVUJhfwTQxVLCHxQRe?tab=summary&timestamp=819.0)
    3.  [When a signal triggers, Leonid contacts the decision-maker to confirm the need.](https://fathom.video/share/qM2aa5qgrqsD-aWVUJhfwTQxVLCHxQRe?tab=summary&timestamp=1762.0)
    4.  [If confirmed, Leonid makes a warm introduction to Alan.](https://fathom.video/share/qM2aa5qgrqsD-aWVUJhfwTQxVLCHxQRe?tab=summary&timestamp=1762.0)

### Pricing & Terms

  - [**Model:** A 3-month pilot retainer, not commission-based.](https://fathom.video/share/qM2aa5qgrqsD-aWVUJhfwTQxVLCHxQRe?tab=summary&timestamp=1080.0)
  - [**Cost:** $3k–$4k per month ($9k–$12k total).](https://fathom.video/share/qM2aa5qgrqsD-aWVUJhfwTQxVLCHxQRe?tab=summary&timestamp=1120.0)
  - [**Rationale:** The long sales cycle (3–6 months) for exec search makes a commission-only model unviable.](https://fathom.video/share/qM2aa5qgrqsD-aWVUJhfwTQxVLCHxQRe?tab=summary&timestamp=1080.0)

### Alan's Skepticism & Request

  - [**Initial Hook:** Leonid's outreach claimed PE firms couldn't find recruiters who understood their business. Alan challenged this, noting the existence of many PE-focused recruiters.](https://fathom.video/share/qM2aa5qgrqsD-aWVUJhfwTQxVLCHxQRe?tab=summary&timestamp=1195.0)
  - [**Leonid's Admission:** Confirmed the hook was a marketing tactic to gain attention, not a specific, recent conversation.](https://fathom.video/share/qM2aa5qgrqsD-aWVUJhfwTQxVLCHxQRe?tab=summary&timestamp=1359.0)
  - [**Market Knowledge:** Alan is already exploring similar automated tools, specifically mentioning `Valley.ai`, which monitors social media for buying signals.](https://fathom.video/share/qM2aa5qgrqsD-aWVUJhfwTQxVLCHxQRe?tab=summary&timestamp=1523.0)
  - [**Request:** Alan needs case studies to validate the service's effectiveness before committing to a follow-up call.](https://fathom.video/share/qM2aa5qgrqsD-aWVUJhfwTQxVLCHxQRe?tab=summary&timestamp=1931.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Leonid:** Send case studies to Alan.](https://fathom.video/share/qM2aa5qgrqsD-aWVUJhfwTQxVLCHxQRe?tab=summary&timestamp=1904.0)
  - [**Alan:** Review case studies to determine if a follow-up call is warranted.](https://fathom.video/share/qM2aa5qgrqsD-aWVUJhfwTQxVLCHxQRe?tab=summary&timestamp=1931.0)
